name: Set-Variable

options.name: -Description
options.description: Specifies the description of the variable.

options.name: -Exclude
options.description: Specifies an array of items that this cmdlet excludes from the operation. The value of this parameter qualifies the Path parameter. Enter a path element or pattern, such as `*.txt`. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Allows you to create a variable with the same name as an existing read-only variable, or to change the value of a read-only variable.

options.name: -Include
options.description: Specifies an array of items that this cmdlet includes in the operation. The value of this parameter qualifies the Name parameter. Enter a name or name pattern, such as `c*`. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -Option
options.description: Specifies the value of the Options property of the variable.

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Returns an object representing the new variable. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the scope of the variable.The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Value
options.description: Specifies the value of the variable.

options.name: -Visibility
options.description: Determines whether the variable is visible outside of the session in which it was created. This parameter is designed for use in scripts and commands that will be delivered to other users.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Sets the value of a variable. Creates the variable if one with the requested name does not exist.
